title: Publisher Confirms, Windows, and Ambiguity · Execution Trace: Confirm Window Bounds
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must enable confirms before publishing, drain confirmation channels, bound outstanding sequence numbers, and reconcile close ambiguity; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the confirm window bounds increment in a bounded Go confirm-window ledger. Learners progres…
objectives: Bound unconfirmed count, bytes, and age while preserving throughput, memory, fairness, and per-message outcome evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Enable confirm mode before publication, continuously consume confirmations, and account for ack, nack, return, close, and unresolved sequence state.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Confirm Window Bounds · Theory Confirm Window Bounds
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 3498 during race-enabled integration test with duplicate delivery. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound outstanding confirms and preserve ambiguity when a channel closes before proof; retain rollback topology before deleting old bindings; then exhaust retry age before attempts reach their maximum. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Bound unconfirmed count, bytes, and age while preserving throughput, memory, fairness, and per-message outcome evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
